name: Solomon's temple
notes: The building, furnishing, and dedication of the house of the LORD, told in Kings and again in Chronicles. The shared core runs from the dimensions of the house, the most holy place and the cherubims overlaid with gold, through the two pillars Jachin and Boaz, the molten sea on twelve oxen, the lavers and candlesticks and vessels, to the bringing up of the ark, the drawing out of the staves, the cloud that filled the house so the priests could not stand to minister, Solomon's blessing and prayer, and the great feast. Each account keeps its own vantage: Kings frames the work with the word of the LORD to Solomon and gives the chambers and doors in fuller detail, while Chronicles adds the Levitical singers as one sound of praise (2Chr 5:11-13), the descent of fire and the glory at the dedication (2Chr 7:1-3), and closes the prayer with 'Arise, O LORD God, into thy resting place.' Where the two record numbers or measures differently, such as the height of the pillars or the capacity of the sea, this is left as a matter for further study; the accounts stand as complementary witnesses to one house and one dedication.
